source.title: Distinct electrophysiological potentials for intention in action and prior intention for action

confidence_rationale: Directly distinguishes proximal (just-in-time) from distal (planned-in-advance) intention using EEG, finding a distinct slow negative frontal 'intention potential' for distal intentions that is separable from the late RP. This is high-quality human EEG with clean experimental dissociation. Transfer risk is moderate because REE's commit token operates over multiple timescales simultaneously, whereas this work cleanly separates them.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Proximal intention (immediately prior to action) and distal intention (formed seconds before movement) are associated with distinct electrophysiological signatures: a late RP differentiates proximal from distal conditions, while a slow negative mid-frontal 'intention potential' appears only for self-paced distal intentions and not for externally-cued distal intentions.
mapping.ree_translation: REE's commit token must encode at minimum two separable temporal registers: a proximal gate (when-to-fire, analogous to late RP) and a distal planning commitment (a prior-intention mark, analogous to the intention potential). A single scalar timestamp is insufficient -- the minimal contract must carry both a 'formed-when' and a 'triggered-when' field to support multi-timescale attribution across E1/E2/E3.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper's distal condition uses a 2.5-second delay between intention formation and movement -- a fixed laboratory window. REE's commit boundary must operate over variable and potentially much longer planning horizons. The mid-frontal 'intention potential' is only present for self-paced decisions, not externally-cued ones; REE must account for authority-triggered commits that may bypass the distal register entirely.
mapping.source_context: Human EEG, voluntary finger movement paradigm, healthy young adults, self-paced vs. externally-cued intention conditions

confidence_components.notes: Clean experimental design with direct manipulation of intention timing. Moderate transfer risk because the REE commit token is a computational construct while the RP/intention-potential are continuous EEG signals; the structural mapping (two distinct temporal registers) is valid but the implementation details differ substantially.
